title: Java Reflection
content:
### Reflection in Java

Reflection allows code to inspect and manipulate classes, methods, fields, and constructors at runtime.

---

### Getting Class Object

```java
// Method 1: Class.forName()
Class<?> c1 = Class.forName("java.lang.String");

// Method 2: .class syntax
Class<?> c2 = String.class;

// Method 3: getClass()
String str = "hello";
Class<?> c3 = str.getClass();
```

---

### Reflection Operations

| Operation | Method |
|-----------|--------|
| Get class name | `getClass().getName()` |
| Get methods | `getMethods()`, `getDeclaredMethods()` |
| Get fields | `getFields()`, `getDeclaredFields()` |
| Get constructors | `getConstructors()` |
| Get annotations | `getAnnotations()` |
| Get superclass | `getSuperclass()` |
| Get interfaces | `getInterfaces()` |
| Get modifiers | `getModifiers()` |

---

### Invoking Methods via Reflection

```java
Class<?> clazz = Class.forName("com.example.MyClass");
Object obj = clazz.getDeclaredConstructor().newInstance();
Method method = clazz.getMethod("myMethod", String.class);
method.invoke(obj, "Hello");
```

---

### Accessing Private Fields

```java
Field field = clazz.getDeclaredField("privateField");
field.setAccessible(true);  // bypass access check
field.set(obj, newValue);
Object value = field.get(obj);
```

---

### Uses and Cautions

**Common Uses:**
- Frameworks (Spring, Hibernate)
- IDE autocompletion
- Testing tools
- Serialization

**Cautions:**
- Performance overhead
- Security restrictions
- Breaks encapsulation
- No compile-time safety

> **Key Point:** Reflection is powerful but should be used sparingly due to performance costs. Always handle `ClassNotFoundException`, `NoSuchMethodException`, `IllegalAccessException`, and `InvocationTargetException`.
